textPlain: I had kids and finally settled into a career at 33. It certainly forced me into adult mode after this.I just met up with my Brother-in-law and his friends for our yearly gathering. All of them are in their 30s and none of them are in what I would consider 'adult mode'.They are all un-married/no kids, barely scraping by, partying every weekend/wasting money on weed and booze. Certainly no careers (mostly retail, some unemployed and still living with parents).I wonder if these numbers will change with the new generation, because so many are not having kids or getting married.
Did you read the article (or hell even the title) before commenting...?
